evals.prompt: Scan the AGENTS.md in a project at E:\dev\auto-trade for coding conventions or design principles that could be promoted to ~/.claude/knowledge/. Apply the promotion gate to each candidate and report results. No file creation needed.
evals.expected_output: A judgment table applying all 5 promotion-gate items to each candidate, organized into three categories: Promote / Decline / SSoT-exception.

assertions.description: Are all 5 promotion-gate items (2+ projects / 6-month durability / project-independent / not a Skill / Why articulable) explicitly applied per candidate?
Is the SSoT exception (AGENTS.md must not be deleted) mentioned?
Are the three outcome categories (Promote / Decline / SSoT-exception) clearly distinguished?

evals.prompt: Read ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and check whether any project-specific content has leaked into the Policy layer (global). Identify anything that should be demoted. No file changes needed.
evals.expected_output: A report assessing each major section of CLAUDE.md as 'appropriate for Policy' or 'project-specific / should be demoted', with demotion rationale where applicable.

assertions.description: Is at least one major section of CLAUDE.md examined?
Where demotion candidates exist, is a reason given for why they should be demoted?

evals.prompt: Run a knowledge-audit on the E:\dev\auto-trade project. Scan AGENTS.md, .claude/commands/, and memory/. Perform promotions and demotions.
evals.expected_output: Promoted knowledge files created or described; a CLAUDE.md trigger-table update proposed; work summary in the required 4-section format (Promoted / Demoted / Declined / Unchanged).

assertions.description: Does the work summary contain all 4 sections: Promoted / Demoted / Promotion declined / Unchanged?
Is there a mention of updating the CLAUDE.md trigger table?
Do the created or proposed knowledge files contain Fact / Rules / Why sections?
